finding.summary: The track has a prominent low-frequency emphasis: 100 Hz band measures -18 dBFS, while 1 kHz is -24 dBFS and 4 kHz is -30 dBFS. This suggests no high-pass filtering and may cause muddiness.
finding.probable_cause: Absence of high-pass filtering; possible proximity effect or low-frequency rumble in the recording.
finding.confidence_reason: 1/3-octave spectrum clearly shows elevated low end relative to mids and highs, but vocal range and genre context are unknown.

evidence_refs.description: 4 kHz band: -30 dBFS (12 dB lower than 100 Hz)

proposal.reason: No corrective EQ or filter plugin exists in the empty FX chain; track volume or pan adjustments cannot address the spectral imbalance. A high-pass filter would be appropriate but cannot be implemented with the available operations.
proposal.goal: Reduce excessive low-frequency buildup below 1 kHz.
